'''Mahe Fonua''' (born 24 December 1992) is a former [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theroar.com.au/2015/09/12/tonga-name-58-man-squad-for-cook-islands-qualifier/|title=Tonga name 58-man squad for Cook Islands qualifier|publisher=theroar.com.au|date=12 September 2015|access-date=30 December 2015}}</ref> [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].
 
He has previously played for the [[Melbourne Storm]] and the [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[NRL]] and [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] in two separate spells in the [[Super League]].

In 2012 he became the first Victorian born and bred player to play for the Melbourne Storm at NRL level.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2015/06/06/victorian_kennar_get.html|title=Victorian Kennar gets his chance|publisher=melbournestorm.com.au|date=6 June 2015|access-date=20 September 2016}}</ref>

==Early life==
Fonua was born in [[Melbourne|Melbourne, Victoria, Australia]]. He was educated at [[Wellington Secondary College]] before moving to Canberra and attending Erindale College in the years of 2008 & 2009 and completing year 12. At the end of 2009 Fonua returned to Victoria where he attended [[Eumemmerring College|Hallam Secondary College]] In the following year repeating year 12 to play for the Hallam School rugby league team.

He played his junior football for the [[South Eastern Titans]] in the [[Victoria Rugby League|Melbourne Rugby League]], before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/team/profiles/mahe-fonua.html|title=Mahe Fonua|website=Melbourne Storm|date=15 April 2015|access-date=15 April 2015|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150419050011/https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/team/profiles/mahe-fonua.html|archive-date=19 April 2015}}</ref>

In 2006 and 2007, Fonua represented the [[NRL Victoria|Victoria Under 15s team]], he also represented the [[NRL Victoria|Victoria Under 18s team]].

In 2008 and 2009, he represented the Australian Capital Territory Under 18s team. He played for the Raiders' [[SG Ball]] team as the Storm did not have an SG Ball team at that time, before returning to [[Melbourne]] after being signed by the [[Melbourne Storm]].

==Playing career==
Fonua played for Melbourne's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team from 2010 to 2012, scoring 32 tries in 45 games.<ref>{{cite web|title=Mahe Fonua|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/f/fonua_mahe/index.php|access-date=10 August 2012|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130116203258/http://rleague.com/db/player/f/fonua_mahe/index.php|archive-date=16 January 2013 }}</ref> At the end of the 2011 season, Fonua was named at {{rlp|ce}} in the 2011 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] Team of the Year.

===2012===
In Round 23 of the [[2012 NRL season]], Fonua made his [[NRL]] debut for Melbourne against the [[Gold Coast Titans]] on the wing in Melbourne's 24–16 win at [[AAMI Park]],<ref>{{cite web|title=Rd 23 Late Mail & Live Chat|url=http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Rd-23-Late-Mail--Live-Chat/61384|access-date=21 August 2013|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131203004450/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Rd-23-Late-Mail--Live-Chat/61384|archive-date=3 December 2013 }}</ref> becoming the first Victorian born and bred player to represent the Storm in first-grade.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/storm-proud-of-hometown-boy-mahe-fonua/story-e6frfgbo-1226447936834|title=Storm Proud of Hometown Boy Mahe Fonua|publisher=Heraldsun.com.au|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref> In Fonua's third game for Melbourne in the Finals 1 week match against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], Fonua scored his first NRL try in the 24–10 win at AAMI Park, after previously being denied tries on four occasions by the [[video referee]], once in his debut, twice in his second game, and once prior to his try in his third game.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/no-storm-cloud-for-stoked-fonua/story-e6frfgbo-1226473752376|title=No Storm Cloud for Stoked Fonua|publisher=Heraldsun.com.au|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref> Fonua finished off his debut year with 4 matches and one a try. On 2 October, Fonua was named at {{rlp|wg}} in the [[Junior Kangaroos]] team to face the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-side-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/70149/default.aspx|title=Junior Kangaroos side announced|date=2 October 2012|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref>

===2013===
Fonua played in Melbourne's [[2013 World Club Challenge]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. He was selected to play for his parents' country of origin [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] on 20 April against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[Pacific Rugby League International#2013 test|Pacific Rugby League International]] at [[Penrith Stadium]]. He scored 2 tries in the 36–4 victory. On 10 June, Fonua extended his contract with Melbourne, keeping at the club till the end of the 2015 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/storm-re-signs-young-talent-9935/|title=Storm re-signs young talent | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2013-06-10|access-date=2015-09-27|archive-date=23 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141023103115/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/storm-re-signs-young-talent-9935/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/melbourne-storm-lock-up-young-talent-including-local-boy-mahe-fonua/story-fni3gpfg-1226659659412|title=Storm Lock Up Young Talent Including Local Boy Mahe Fonua|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref> In Round 21 against Canberra at [[Canberra Stadium]], Fonua scored a hat-trick of tries in the 68–4 win<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2013-08-04/nrl-wrap-sunday-august-4/4863956|title=Melbourne Storm snap Raiders' 12-game home streak with 64-point thrashing |publisher=ABC News |location=Australia|date=2013-08-04|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref> including a spectacular set up for [[Will Chambers]] after the ball seemed almost certain to go dead, with some commentators comparing it to the famous set up by [[Greg Inglis]] during the centenary test.<ref>{{cite web|last=Ryan|first=Nathan|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/freakish-mahe-fonua-the-new-greg-inglis-following-try-assist-heroics-in-melbourne-victory/story-fndv325j-1226691288506|title=Freakish Mahe Fonua the new Greg Inglis following try-assist heroics in Melbourne victory|publisher=News.com.au|date=2013-08-05|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref> Fonua was selected in the [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] 24 man [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] squad, playing in 2 of their 3 matches.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.triplem.com.au/sydney/sport/nrl/news/2013/10/tonga-names-rugby-league-world-cup-2013-squad/|title=Tonga Names Rugby League World Cup 2013 Squad | News|publisher=Triple M|date=2013-10-08|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref>

===2014===
Fonua was selected in Melbourne's inaugural [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|access-date=2015-09-27|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=15 January 2016}}</ref> He finished Melbourne's [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing in 20 matches and scoring 10 tries.

===2015===
On 2 May, Fonua played on the wing for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] 16–18 loss in the [[Pacific Rugby League International#Polynesian Cup|Polynesian Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/tonga-named-face-samoa-2015-pacific-test|title=Tonga Named To Face Samoa in Pacific Test|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-04-26|access-date=2015-09-27|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203180119/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/tonga-named-face-samoa-2015-pacific-test/|archive-date=3 February 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/video-sam-kasiano-try-saving-tackle-on-jorge-taufua-secures-pacific-test-win-for-samoan-against-tonga/story-e6frf3ou-1227331678892|title=Video : Samoa v Tonga|publisher=Foxpsorts.com.au|access-date=2015-09-27}}</ref> He finished his last season with the Melbourne Storm with 13 matches and 4 tries. On 17 October, he represented Tonga again, this time in their [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification (Asia-Pacific play-off)|Asia-Pacific Qualifier]] match against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].

===2016===
On 1 August, he signed a three-year contract with [[Super League]] team [[Hull F.C.]] starting in 2016.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/33743819|title = Hull FC sign Fonua on three-year deal|publisher = BBC Sport}}</ref> Fonua had an outstanding first season with the club and quickly established himself as a fan favourite. He helped Hull F.C. reach the [[2016 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup Final]] where they would go on to beat the [[Warrington Wolves]] by a narrow score of 12–10.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title = Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher = BBC Sport}}</ref> Fonua scored the first of Hull FC's two tries at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

In his first season in the [[Super League]], he scored 15 tries which contributed to him being selected for the [[Super League Dream Team#2016|Super League Dream Team]] along with 5 other Hull teammates.

===2017===

On 9 June 2017, it was confirmed that Fonua would return to the NRL, to join the [[Wests Tigers]], starting in the 2018 season.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tigers/wests-tigers-sign-former-nrl-wingers-corey-thompson-and-mahe-fonua-from-2018/news-story/a359a6adfe6d44209844300c0b4ad63d|title=Wests Tigers sign former NRL wingers Corey Thompson and Mahe Fonua from 2018|date=2017-06-08|publisher=Fox Sports|access-date=2017-06-08|language=en-GB}}</ref>

On 26 August 2017, Fonua would go on to win the [[2017 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] for a second year in a row, contributing massively to the 18–14 win over the [[Wigan Warriors]] by scoring two of Hull's three tries at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|work=Daily Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Fonua finished the year with making the 2017 Super League Dream Team for a second year in a row, but this time as a winger.

===2018===

At the start of the [[2018 NRL season]], Fonua spent the first few weeks playing for [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]] in the [[Intrust Super Premiership NSW]].  After a number of good performances, Fonua was called up to play for the [[Wests Tigers]].  Fonua made his debut for the club in their 22–20 defeat [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]]. He played two games on the wing before being switched to centre. On 15 May, Fonua was demoted to reserve grade for showing up late to training, days after a Man of the Match performance.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/wests-tigers-cult-hero-mahe-fonuna-dropped-to-reserve-grade-for-being-late-to-training/news-story/2821de917dd82bdf8c9a7f586b513409|title = Wests Tigers cult hero Mahe Fonuna dropped to reserve grade for being late to training|date = 15 May 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2018/03/22/magpie-fonua-prepares-nrl-swoop/|title = Magpie Fonua prepares NRL swoop|date = 22 March 2018}}</ref> Weeks later, he was sidelined after breaking his arm,<ref>{{cite web| work= Sporting News | title=Wests Tigers cult hero Mahe Fonua's season in doubt with broken arm | url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/mahe-fonua-injury-wests-tigers-nrl-canberra-raiders/rf5nx7cvf8mz1jrhiudxzvpd4}}</ref> but made a return later in the year.

Despite only scoring one try from his ten appearances, Fonua lead the club and was fifth in the NRL for post-contact metres.<ref>{{cite web| publisher= National Rugby League | title=Wests Tigers: 2018 season by the numbers|author=Chris Kennedy | date=November 2018| url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/11/01/wests-tigers-2018-season-review-by-the-numbers/}}</ref> He also gained attention for his [[Mullet (haircut)|mullet haircut]].<ref>{{cite news| work= The Sydney Morning Herald | title=Tigers give 'Mullet Man' Mahe the chop after late start|author=Christian Nicolussi | url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/tigers-give-mullet-man-mahe-the-chop-after-late-start-20180515-p4zfc7.html}}</ref>

===2019===
In the [[2019 NRL season]], Fonua suffered a season ending injury.  Fonua later announced his return to Super League side [[Hull FC]].

===2020===
Fonua played 17 games for [[Hull FC]] in the [[2020 Super League season]] including the club's semi-final loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-hull-fc-highlights-4716172#964303/|title=Wigan Warriors v Hull FC highlights as FC struggle to find play-off form in one-sided defeat|website=hulldailymail.co.uk|date=19 November 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 9 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Fonua scored two tries for Hull F.C. in a 30–12 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. On 8 October it was announced that Fonua had been released by Hull FC with a year left on his contract. On 13 October 2021, Castleford Tigers confirmed the signing of Fonua on an initial one-year deal with the option of a further two years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12329593/super-league-adam-swift-and-mahe-fonua-set-hull-fc-on-course-for-30-12-win-over-castleford-tigers/|title= Adam Swift and Mahe Fonua set Hull FC on course for 30-12 win over Castleford Tigers|publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref>

=== 2022 ===
Fonua was assigned squad number 17 for the [[Super League XXVII|2022 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-01-21 |title=Tigers reveal 2022 squad numbers |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7841 |access-date=2022-03-12 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He made his [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] debut on 11 February against the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the opening round of Super League.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-02-11 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-26 Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60320580 |access-date=2022-03-12 |publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first try for the club on 12 March against the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-03-12 |title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants 36-24 Castleford Tigers – Ricky Leutele runs in hat-trick |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60681568 |access-date=2022-03-12 |publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===2023===
On 4 August 2023 it was announced that he, along with [[Bureta Faraimo]] would join [[Doncaster RLFC]] for the remainder of the 2023 season, following the pair's release from [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-released-overseas-duo-join-league-1-outfit-for-remainder-of-season|title=Castleford Tigers: Released overseas duo join League 1 outfit for remainder of season|website=Love Rugby League|date=4 Aug 2023}}</ref>

===2025===
On 12 November 2025 he announced his retirement<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=12 Nov 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Castleford Tigers and Hull FC back Mahe Fonua retires|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-castleford-tigers-and-hull-fc-back-mahe-fonua-retires/}}</ref>
